apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of encoding schemes to solve real-world problems.
Students will be able to apply the concepts of number systems to convert numbers from one system to another.
Students will be able to use the knowledge of number systems to perform arithmetic operations on numbers in different systems.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the advantages and disadvantages of different encoding schemes.
Students will be able to analyze the relationships between different number systems and identify the most suitable system for a given problem.
Students will be able to analyze the performance of different number systems in terms of storage and processing requirements.
remember: Students will be able to recall and list different types of encoding schemes such as ASCII, Unicode, and EBCDIC.
Students will be able to remember the basic principles of binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal number systems.
Students will be able to recall the conversion methods between different number systems.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of encoding schemes and their importance in computer systems.
Students will be able to comprehend the binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal number systems and their relationships.
Students will be able to understand the process of conversion between different number systems.

ncert_objectives: Explain the need for and types of encoding schemes.
Determine the number system used by a computer system.
Convert numbers from one number system to another.
board_exam_objectives: Define and explain ASCII, Unicode, and EBCDIC encoding schemes.
Explain the binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal number systems.
Convert numbers from decimal to binary, octal, and hexadecimal, and vice versa.
